summary: Matched and cleaned up ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag by replacing a temporary SOLUTION/M2C_FIELD-based implementation with named Fighter and ftCommonData fields for SDI behavior. The PR also propagated the new SDI names across damage, guard, collision, hitlag, and fighter lifecycle code, turning opaque fields x221A_b2, x4B0, x4B4, and x4B8 into allow_sdi, sdi_min_stick_mag, sdi_stick_window, and sdi_pos_scale.
what_changed: Removed the local `#define SOLUTION 1` branch scaffolding from `ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag`.

Rewrote `ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag` to use `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)`, `allow_sdi`, `VEC2_SQ_LEN`, and `SQ` instead of direct `gobj->user_data`, manual stick magnitude math, and alternate `M2C_FIELD` access paths.
Changed the SDI input threshold check from equality against `x4B0 * x4B0` to `VEC2_SQ_LEN(fp->input.lstick) >= SQ(p_ftCommonData->sdi_min_stick_mag)`.
Named `ftCommonData` fields at offsets +4B0, +4B4, and +4B8 as `sdi_min_stick_mag`, `sdi_stick_window`, and `sdi_pos_scale`.
Named Fighter bitfield `x221A_b2` as `allow_sdi` and updated all shown uses in damage, guard, fighter, and collision-adjacent code.
Updated hitlag/SDI state setup and teardown to set or clear `allow_sdi` instead of `x221A_b2`.
Updated guard and c-stick SDI-related threshold checks to use `sdi_min_stick_mag`, `sdi_stick_window`, and `sdi_pos_scale` where appropriate.
Kept the timer reset value for stick tilt timers as decimal `254`, replacing previous `0xFE` spelling in the matched function.
smart_moves: Converted unclear offset and bitfield names into semantic SDI names, making the matched function read like gameplay logic rather than reverse-engineering scaffolding.
Removed the temporary `SOLUTION` macro and duplicate conditional code paths after resolving the struct field names, reducing maintenance risk.
Used existing math/helper macros (`VEC2_SQ_LEN`, `SQ`) to express squared vector magnitude checks compactly and consistently.
Propagated the `allow_sdi` rename across all dependent code in the same PR, avoiding a partially named state bit that would be harder to search and reason about.

Renamed common data fields based on their observed usage across damage, guard, and c-stick checks, strengthening confidence that the names describe shared SDI parameters rather than one local behavior.
decomp_lessons: When a temporary match uses `M2C_FIELD` for known offsets, replacing it with real struct fields can be the final step needed to make the decompilation clean and reviewable.

A bitfield should be named from all of its behavioral uses, not only its storage location; here the same flag gates hitlag completion, collision behavior, guard setoff handling, and SDI movement, supporting `allow_sdi`.
Common-data constants become much easier to validate when renamed by cross-file usage patterns: magnitude threshold, stick-window timer, and position scale all appeared in multiple SDI/ASDI contexts.
Prefer squared-length helpers and `SQ()` for stick threshold code to avoid unnecessary square roots and to mirror common code style.
Once a struct field name is established, update nearby callsites in the same patch so future matching work can search for the semantic name instead of stale `xNNNN` placeholders.

naming_conventions.rationale: Flag gates whether SDI/hitlag-related movement and special collision handling are allowed.
Used as the minimum analog/c-stick magnitude threshold for SDI checks.
Compared against stick tilt timers `x670_timer_lstick_tilt_x` and `x671_timer_lstick_tilt_y`.
Scales stick input into SDI position displacement.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Eliminated alternate `#if SOLUTION` paths after determining that direct named bitfield/timer access could match.
Replaced manual `lstick_x * lstick_x + lstick_y * lstick_y` with `VEC2_SQ_LEN(fp->input.lstick)` while preserving squared-magnitude semantics.
Used `SQ(p_ftCommonData->sdi_min_stick_mag)` for threshold comparisons, matching the existing style also used in `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag` and `ftCo_800DF608`.
Simplified nested `if` structure in `ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag` into one compound condition, relying on named fields to keep the generated code and intent clear.
Used `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` in the matched function instead of direct `gobj->user_data`, consistent with many fighter functions in the tree.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR slice.
The provided context indicates one issue comment and one review, but their text was not available in the excerpt, so no reviewer rationale can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining uses of `x4BC` and `x4C0` near guard SDI/ASDI behavior to determine whether they also deserve SDI-related names.
Search for remaining `x221A_b3` usage around damage knockback and hitlag to name the adjacent bitfield.
Audit other `ftCommonData` fields around offsets +4A0 to +4C4 for SDI, ASDI, shield, or hitlag parameter names.
Check whether decimal `254` versus `0xFE` is preferred style for timer sentinel values elsewhere in fighter code.
